rationale: The cited 2001 paper appears foundational rather than incidental: it is described as one of the first empirical tests linking brand attitude changes to abnormal stock returns and future ROE. The 2013 citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior marketing-finance work on mean brand evaluations and studies related outcomes (abnormal returns/risk, firm value), making substantive use plausible. With an exact DOI match and strong conceptual overlap, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
